From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Not to be confused with [Artistic director](/wiki/Artistic_director "Artistic director").

Supervisor of an artistic production

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **This article has multiple issues.** Please help **[improve it](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Art_director "Special:EditPage/Art director")** or discuss these issues on the **[talk page](/wiki/Talk:Art_director "Talk:Art director")**. *([Learn how and when to remove these messages](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  | | --- | --- | | [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Art_director "Special:EditPage/Art director") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Art director"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Art+director%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Art+director%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Art+director%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Art+director%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Art+director%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Art+director%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | This article **may contain [original research](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")**. Please [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit) by [verifying](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") the claims made and adding [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources"). Statements consisting only of original research should be removed. *(August 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Book_Cover_for_Thoughts_on_Design_by_Paul_Rand.jpg/250px-Book_Cover_for_Thoughts_on_Design_by_Paul_Rand.jpg)](/wiki/File:Book_Cover_for_Thoughts_on_Design_by_Paul_Rand.jpg)

*Thoughts on Design,* a book by influential art director and [graphic designer](/wiki/Graphic_designer "Graphic designer") [Paul Rand](/wiki/Paul_Rand "Paul Rand")

**Art director** is a title for a variety of similar job functions in [advertising](/wiki/Advertising "Advertising"), [marketing](/wiki/Marketing "Marketing"), [publishing](/wiki/Publishing "Publishing"), the [performing arts](/wiki/Performing_arts "Performing arts") (including [theater](/wiki/Theatre "Theatre"), [film](/wiki/Film_industry "Film industry"), [television](/wiki/Television "Television"), and [animation](/wiki/Animation "Animation")), fashion, the [Internet](/wiki/Internet "Internet"), and [video games](/wiki/Video_game "Video game").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The art director's role is to supervise the visual style and images of an artistic production.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In particular, they are in charge of its overall visual appearance and how it communicates visually, stimulates moods, contrasts features, and psychologically appeals to a target audience. The art director makes decisions about visual elements, what artistic [style(s)](/wiki/Style_(visual_arts) "Style (visual arts)") to use, and when to use [motion](/wiki/Motion_graphic_design "Motion graphic design"). One of the biggest challenges art directors face is translating desired moods, messages, concepts, and underdeveloped ideas into imagery. In the brainstorm process, art directors, colleagues and clients explore ways the finished piece or scene could look. At times, the art director is responsible for solidifying the vision of the collective imagination while resolving conflicting agendas and inconsistencies between contributors' ideas.

In eastern animated works, such as Japanese [anime](/wiki/Anime "Anime") and [Chinese animation](/wiki/Chinese_animation "Chinese animation"), the role of art director (美術監督, *bijutsu kantoku*) specifically refers to the artist in charge of supervising and directing the background art and the background art staff of a particular work, rather than a role unifying a work's overall artistic vision.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## In advertising and marketing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: In advertising and marketing")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion** with: content on an art director's role in marketing. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=). *(August 2025)* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2d/Art_director_and_copy_writer_at_a_large_advertising_agency._Detroit%2C_Michigan._July_1942._%2826767552717%29.jpg/250px-Art_director_and_copy_writer_at_a_large_advertising_agency._Detroit%2C_Michigan._July_1942._%2826767552717%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Art_director_and_copy_writer_at_a_large_advertising_agency._Detroit,_Michigan._July_1942._(26767552717).jpg)

Art director and copywriter at an [advertising agency](/wiki/Advertising_agency "Advertising agency") in Detroit, Michigan, in 1942

In modern [advertising](/wiki/Advertising "Advertising") practice, an art director typically works with a [copywriter](/wiki/Copywriting "Copywriting") as a creative team[[5]](#cite_note-5) to devise an overall concept (also known as the "creative" or "big idea") for a commercial, mailer, brochure, or other advertisements. The copywriter is responsible for the text (referred to as "[copy](/wiki/Copy_(publishing) "Copy (publishing)")"), and the art director for the visual aspects.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Art directors commonly collaborate on execution of the advertisement with other team members such as [graphic designers](/wiki/Graphic_designer "Graphic designer").[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7) A benefit of a creative team is that art directors may contribute to the concept or copy, and copywriters may suggest a visual approach. Such collaboration, first pioneered by [Bill Bernbach](/wiki/William_Bernbach "William Bernbach") of [DDB](/wiki/DDB_Worldwide "DDB Worldwide"), is considered to improve the creative work.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Although a good art director is expected to have good [graphic design](/wiki/Graphic_design "Graphic design") judgment and technical knowledge of production, it may *not* be necessary for an art director to [hand-render](/wiki/Artistic_rendering "Artistic rendering") [comprehensive layouts](/wiki/Comprehensive_layout "Comprehensive layout"), or even be able to draw, now that virtually all but the most preliminary work is done on computer. Despite the title, an advertising art director is not necessarily the head of an art department.

A team composed of an art director and copywriter is typically overseen by a [creative director](/wiki/Creative_director "Creative director").[[7]](#cite_note-:0-7) In a large organization, an art director may oversee other art directors and a team of junior [designers](/wiki/Graphic_designer "Graphic designer"), [image developers](/wiki/Visual_arts "Visual arts") and/or [production artists](/wiki/Production_artist "Production artist"), and coordinate with a separate production department. In a smaller organization, the art director may fill all these roles, including overseeing printing and other production.

## Performing arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Performing arts")]

### In theater

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In theater")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=). *(August 2025)* |

### In film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: In film")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4a/Bahubali_With_Prabhas.jpg/250px-Bahubali_With_Prabhas.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bahubali_With_Prabhas.jpg)

Actor [Prabhas](/wiki/Prabhas "Prabhas") with [Sabu Cyril](/wiki/Sabu_Cyril "Sabu Cyril"), production designer and art director for *[Baahubali: The Beginning](/wiki/Baahubali:_The_Beginning "Baahubali: The Beginning")*

An art director heads a film [art department](/wiki/Art_department "Art department"), working directly below the [production designer](/wiki/Production_designer "Production designer") and in collaboration with the set decorator and the set designers.[[11]](#cite_note-:1-11) A large part of their duties include the administrative aspects of the art department. They are responsible for assigning tasks to personnel such as the art department coordinator and the construction coordinator, keeping track of the art department budget and scheduling, and overall quality control. They are often also a liaison to other departments, especially construction, special effects, property, transportation (graphics), and locations departments. The art director also attends all production meetings and tech scouts in order to provide information to the set designers in preparation for all departments to have a visual floor plan of each location visited.

The term "art director" was first used in 1914 by Wilfred Buckland[[12]](#cite_note-12) when this title was used to denote the head of the art department (hence the [Academy Award for Best Art Direction](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Production_Design "Academy Award for Best Production Design")), which also included the set decorator. Now the award includes the production designer and set decorator. On the movie *[Gone with the Wind](/wiki/Gone_with_the_Wind_(film) "Gone with the Wind (film)")*, [David O. Selznick](/wiki/David_O._Selznick "David O. Selznick") felt that [William Cameron Menzies](/wiki/William_Cameron_Menzies "William Cameron Menzies") had such a significant role in the look of the film that the title art director was not sufficient, and so he gave Menzies the title of production designer.[[13]](#cite_note-13) This title is now commonly used as the title for the head of the art department, although the title actually implies control over every visual aspect of a film, including costumes.

On films with smaller art departments, such as small independent films and short films, the terms "production designer" and "art director" are often synonymous, and the person taking on the role may be credited as either.[[11]](#cite_note-:1-11)

In the United States and Canada, the [Art Directors Guild](/wiki/Art_Directors_Guild "Art Directors Guild") is a [labor union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") for art directors and related professions in film and television production.

### In television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: In television")]

## In publishing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Art_director&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: In publishing")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a0/Louise_Fili_and_Gail_Anderson_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Louise_Fili_and_Gail_Anderson_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Louise_Fili_and_Gail_Anderson_(cropped).jpg)

[Louise Fili](/wiki/Louise_Fili "Louise Fili"), former art director at [Pantheon Books](/wiki/Pantheon_Books "Pantheon Books"), and [Gail Anderson](/wiki/Gail_Anderson_(graphic_designer) "Gail Anderson (graphic designer)"), former senior art director of *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*

Art directors in [publishing](/wiki/Publishing "Publishing") typically work with the publication's [editors](/wiki/Editing "Editing"). Together, they work on a concept for sections and pages of a publication. The art director is mostly responsible for the visual look and feel of the publication, and the editor has ultimate responsibility for the publication's text content.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Mehemed Fehmy Agha](/wiki/Mehemed_Fehmy_Agha "Mehemed Fehmy Agha") was one of the first art directors in [magazine](/wiki/Magazine "Magazine") publishing, having assumed the role first at German *Vogue* in [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin"), and then in 1929 at [Condé Nast](/wiki/Cond%C3%A9_Nast "Condé Nast") in New York, providing art direction for *[Vanity Fair](/wiki/Vanity_Fair_(magazine) "Vanity Fair (magazine)")*, [*Vogue*](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)"), and [*House & Garden*](/wiki/House_%26_Garden_(magazine) "House & Garden (magazine)").[[14]](#cite_note-14)
